title: Synthetic support-message template
description: Records resemble inbound customer-support messages. Use for support-classifier / routing eval inputs. No real PII per /skills/synthetic-data-generator/SKILL.md privacy guardrails.

message_id.description: Synthetic ID; do not collide with real production ID format.

channel.description: Inbound channel. Distribution defaults to: email 40%, in_app_chat 30%, support_form 15%, twitter_dm 10%, phone_transcript 5%.

tenant_id.description: Synthetic tenant id. Generator never reuses tenant ids that appear in /context/.

customer_handle.description: Display name OR username. Synthetic — never matches real-customer or real-public-figure names. Use plausible-but-fake names mixing regions.

urgency_hint.description: Optional. ~50% of records include this; a quarter of those are 'ambiguous'.

category_seed.description: Diversity-control hint for the generator. Default split: billing 30%, technical 30%, account_access 15%, feature_request 10%, complaint 10%, other 5%.

body.description: Free-text message body. Length distribution: short (<50 words) 25%, medium (50-200) 60%, long (200+) 15%. Tone register varies (terse / neutral / verbose). Must NOT contain real-format SSNs, credit card numbers, real phone numbers, or real-customer-domain emails.

thread_position.description: 1 = first message in thread; > 1 = follow-up. ~80% of records are first-message; 20% are follow-ups for thread-aware testing.

_diversity_profile.comment: Read by the generator at planning time. Removed from the schema-validation copy.
